name: Tom Brown
key_contributions: Lead author of "Language Models are Few-Shot Learners" (2020) — the GPT-3 paper
GPT-3 demonstrated that scaling language models to 175B parameters unlocked emergent few-shot learning abilities
The GPT-3 paper established the scaling paradigm that drove the field toward ever-larger models
Showed that large language models could perform tasks via in-context learning without fine-tuning
GPT-3 directly led to ChatGPT and the generative AI explosion
summary: Tom Brown was the lead author of the GPT-3 paper ('Language Models are Few-Shot Learners,' 2020), one of the most influential papers in modern AI. GPT-3 demonstrated that scaling a language model to 175 billion parameters unlocked remarkable few-shot learning abilities — the model could perform tasks it had never been explicitly trained for simply by being given a few examples in the prompt. This result established the scaling paradigm that dominated AI research and directly led to ChatGPT, Claude, and the generative AI revolution.
